_comment: Multi-angle complexity-stratified validation of SAGE on the 91-Peplink-patent full-text corpus. Same sled as baselines.json; new query set (queries_complexity.json) that systematically varies complexity along 8 monotone-difficulty levels.

complexity_taxonomy.3_single_filter: 1 constraint — assignee OR inventor OR CPC
complexity_taxonomy.5_triple_filter: 3 constraints intersect (assignee AND keyword AND CPC)
complexity_taxonomy.6_bridge_2hop: Chain reasoning — find X through entity Y
complexity_taxonomy.7_multi_entity_enum: 'Two/Three X' style — broad GT, any match counts

key_findings.monotonic_difficulty_not_strict: MRR doesn't monotonically decrease with complexity. Level 3 (single filter) is the EASIEST (MRR=1.000); level 4 (dual filter) is the HARDEST direct query (MRR=0.583). Level 5 (triple filter) rebounds to 0.900 because the query becomes specific enough to lock onto the right entity cluster.
key_findings.bridge_and_enumeration_strong: Level 6 (bridge / 2-hop reasoning) and Level 7 (multi-entity enumeration) both hit MRR=0.900. The system's multi-hop machinery WORKS on real production patents.
key_findings.dual_filter_weakness: Level 4 ('Pismo Labs patents about bonding') is the soft spot — assignee + topic combos get diluted because the topic-keyword tends to dominate the addressing score over the assignee entity match. Future improvement: weighted constraint composition.
key_findings.system_is_robust: Worst-case MRR across all 8 complexity levels = 0.583. No level catastrophically fails. The system handles every complexity tier with first-correct-hit at rank ≤ 2 on average.

regression_thresholds._comment: If any complexity level's MRR drops below the floor here, a regression has landed.
